# 📊 Telecom Call Data Analytics & ML Project

## 📌 Overview
End-to-end data analytics and machine learning project on **1M+ Telecom Call Detail Records (CDR)** to analyze user behavior, extract insights, and build predictive models using Python.

---

## 🎯 Objective
- Analyze telecom call patterns and user behavior  
- Identify peak usage hours and revenue trends  
- Detect high-cost calls and network issues  
- Build ML models for prediction tasks  

---

## 📂 Dataset
- **1,000,000+ call records**  
- Includes call time, duration, type, direction, tower info, roaming status, and cost  

---

## ⚙️ Workflow
- Data Cleaning & Preprocessing  
- Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)  
- Feature Engineering (time-based + categorical features)  
- Machine Learning Modeling  
- Model Evaluation  

---

## 📊 Key Insights
- Peak usage occurs during evening hours  
- Small user group generates majority of revenue  
- Majority calls are short-duration  
- Certain towers show high congestion  
- Revenue strongly linked with call volume  

---

## 🤖 Machine Learning Models
- **LightGBM Classifier** → High-cost call detection  
- **LightGBM Regressor** → Call cost prediction  
- Evaluation using **Accuracy, AUC, and RMSE**  

---

## 🛠️ Tools & Libraries
Python | Pandas | NumPy | Matplotlib | Seaborn | Scikit-learn | LightGBM  

---

## 🚀 Outcome
Delivered actionable telecom insights and predictive models for call cost analysis and network optimization.
